calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-24-2023 Off Calendar

Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Default Judgment Prove Up Hearing, Points And Authorities, Declaration In Suppor Of Motion
Off Calendar Mar-01-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for motion for default judgment prove-up hearing.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 11:21:08 a.m. and concluding at 11:46:27 a.m.

Plaintiff Sunstate Equipment Co., LLC represented by Lorna Walker, Esq. via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Eagle Enviornmental & Construction, a Corporation, DBA Eagle Environmental Construction not appearing.

ANDY EVANS (Director - Credit & Collections) is sworn to testify on behalf of Plaintiff.

Court ordered matter continued from Jan-17-2023 for testimony of witness with personal knowledge of accounting.

Court examines ANDY EVANS (Director - Credit & Collections).

The following exhibits are MARKED/ADMITTED INTO EVIDENCE:

Plaintiff's Exhibit No. B - Account Information Sheet
Plaintiff's Exhibit No. C - Four Week Bill
Plaintiff's Exhibit No. A - Customer Invoice Inquiry

Court examines Plaintiff's Counsel regarding calculation of attorney fees.
Court determines DOES 1-20 were DISMISSED on Oct-27-2022.

The matter is submitted.
Based on the summary of the case, comments made by Plaintiff's witness and its Counsel, and evidence presented, the Court finds that Plaintiff has proven each essential element of the complaint.

Good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff Sunstate Equipment Co., LLC, and against Defendant Eagle Enviornmental & Construction, a Corporation, DBA Eagle Environmental Construction, as follows:

Plaintiff's request for pre-judgment interest is DENIED. Court finds rate to be accessive and unconscionable.

Plaintiff's request for attorney fees is GRANTED based on the local rules and Plaintiff's alledgment in its complaint.

1. Damages: $106,944.99
2. Attorney Fees: $2,825.00
3. Costs: $757.00

TOTAL JUDGMENT: $110,526.99

Plaintiff further presents argument in support of its request for pre-judgment interest pursuant to Civil Code Section 3289.

The matter is submitted.
Plaintiff's request for pre-judgment interest remains DENIED. Plaintiff's request is beyond Civil Code Section 3289.

Plaintiff further requests at least 10% pre-judgment interest be awarded.
Plaintiff's further request for 10% pre-judgment interest is GRANTED IN PART. Court hereby awards 7% Pre-Judgment Intererest. Plaintiff's request for Statement of Decision pursuant to Civil Code of Procedure 632 is DENIED.

Court modifies judgment as follows:

1. Damages: $106,944.99
2. Attorney Fees: $2,825.00
3. Costs: $757.00
4. Interest: $21,412.44

TOTAL JUDGMENT: $131,939.43

Proposed judgment signed as modified.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-06-2023 Continued To Apr-19-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Default Judgment Prove Up Hearing, Points And Authorities, Declaration In Suppor Of Motion
Off Calendar Jan-17-2023 Continued To Mar-01-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 505
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for motion for default judgment prove-up hearing.

Dept. 514; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 10:37:25 a.m. and concluding at 10:56:12 a.m.

Plaintiff Sunstate Equipment Co., LLC, a Limited Liability Company represented by Lorna Walker, Esq.
Defendant Eagle Environmental & Construction, a Corporation, DBA Eagle Environmental Construction not appearing.

AARON HEMSTALK is sworn to testify on behalf of Plaintiff.

Counsel for Plaintiff presents summary of case and an offer of proof.

The following exhibits are MARKED/ADMITTED INTO EVIDENCE:

Plaintiff's Exhibit A - Account Statements
Plaintiff's Exhibit B - Credit Application
Plaintiff's Exhibit C - Invoice for Equipment

AARON HEMSTALK is examined by the Court.

Court finds testimony of Aaron Hemstalk insufficient.
Court requires testimony of the custodian records for accounting.

Court orders matter continued to Mar-01-2023 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 505 for testimony of custodian of records and further hearing on motion for default judgment prove-up hearing.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
